feedback.text: I do support the calls to preserve our purchases of videogames expressed in other commentary. If possible, i would also like to raise concern about another issue that has appeared recently in public space - the fact that payment processing companies apparently can decide what type of content is being sold by platforms like steam or itch.io - content that is legal under the law, but simply being viewed unfavorably by those payment processors. I believe those companies should not have a say in what type of content they process transactions for as long as the content itself is legal under the law. While they are private companies unfortunately they have such a stranglehold on internet commerce that they really should be treated and regulated like public services.
